programme.title: Archdiocesan Engagement for Mission Programme (AEMP)
programme.description: Leadership and spiritual formation initiative to deepen spiritual commitment, evangelization capacity, and leadership effectiveness of lay faithful and clergy.
purpose: Enhance parish and lay group participation in the Church's mission
Deepen spirituality and discernment of God-given talents
Prepare lay Catholics for formal leadership roles (pipeline of successors by 2030)
Promote personal growth applicable to all areas of life
programme.mandatory: Required for anyone seeking formal leadership positions in the Archdiocese

assessmentCriteria.plagiarismPolicy: Strictly penalized (zero scores for both copier and source); may be reported to parish priests

prayer.title: AEMP Prayer
prayer.fullText: Heavenly Father, we Your children of the Catholic Archdiocese of Lagos entrust ourselves and the Archdiocesan Engagement for Mission Programme into Your Hands. Grant us Your wisdom and discernment, as we navigate the changes and challenges that lie ahead. Bless the work of our hands, that the AEMP may contribute to the well-being, growth and salvation of all. May we, with the help of the Holy Spirit, contribute to this endeavor with integrity, creativity, and perseverance. Grant us your Spirit of Unity. Help us to recognize and put aside our selfish desires and work together in harmony towards our common goals of spiritual-commitment and engagement. May our words be filled with love and respect, and our actions reflect Your grace. Give us courage and strength to overcome any anxieties that keep us from accepting the changes needed in this programme. Help us to move forward with peace in our hearts during and after this transition. When we are daunted by the pressures of the AEMP, help us to depend on your strength and increase our faith. We ask all these through Jesus Christ, our Lord. Amen. Our Lady, Seat of Wisdom, Pray for Us. Our Lady, Queen of Nigeria, Pray for Us. Our Lady, Queen of Peace, Pray for Us.

topics.name: Parish Guidelines (Topic 15)
assignments: Read 'St. Thomas Parish Winneba' case study and respond
Read Parish Pastoral & Laity Guidelines - compare with your parish operations (4 aligned + 4 non-aligned for each council)
Read Parish Finance Council & Administration Guidelines - compare with your parish (4 aligned + 4 non-aligned)
Read Human Resources Management Guidelines - compare for laity and clergy (4 aligned + 4 non-aligned each)
Reflect on how guidelines help holiness and evangelization (what to do more/less/start/stop)

topics.name: Developing Self-Awareness for Mission
assignments: Complete 6 Developing Self-Awareness Diagnostic Surveys (Excel sheets)
Identify Top 5 Gallup Talent Themes using CliftonStrengths assessment
Discover Top 3 Spiritual Gifts via spiritualgiftsinventory.com
Read about spiritual gifts from provided PDF resource
Complete 'Looking Through the Glass' exercise
Reflect on self-awareness scenario, emotional intelligence, cognitive styles, tolerance of ambiguity, locus of control
Analyze your Gallup Top Five Talents by domain (Executing/Influencing/Relationship Building/Strategic Thinking)
Identify Top 3 Ministry Spiritual Gifts by category (Roots/Branches/Leaves/Water)

topics.name: Managing Personal Stress for Mission
assignments: Complete Managing Personal Stress Diagnostic Surveys (6 sheets)
Review current stress/time management competence levels and set 12-month goals
Identify current stressors and coping strategies (eliminate vs. manage)
Read and respond to 'Mabel' case study on stress management

topics.name: Solving Problems for Mission
assignments: Complete 3 Solving Problems Diagnostic Surveys
Review analytical/creative problem-solving competence levels and set 12-month goals
Study 'A Model of Problem Solving' handout
Apply problem-solving model to 'St. Peter's Catholic Church' case study
Respond to 'Father Tom's Laptop' case questions

resources.slides: AEMP - Solving Problems for Mission - May 2026.pdf

topics.name: Communicating Supportively for Mission
assignments: Complete 'Communicating Supportively' Diagnostic Survey
Read 'The Eight Principles of Supportive Communication' - Coaching vs Counseling
Analyze 'Cases involving Building Positive Relationships'

resources.slides: AEMP - Communicating Supportively for Mission - May 2026.pdf

topics.name: Gaining Credibility and Influence for Mission
assignments: Complete 'Gaining Power and Influence' Diagnostic Survey
Analyze 'The New Parish Laity Council Chairman' case study
Answer questions on power (pros/cons), personal vs position power
Identify parish positions with centrality, flexibility, visibility, relevance
Reflect on transforming power into influence

resources.slides: AEMP - Gaining Credibility and Influence for Mission - May 2026.pdf

topics.name: Managing Conflicts for Mission
assignments: Complete 'Managing Conflicts' Diagnostic Survey
Reflect on personal instances of conflict (self, authorities, collaborators, faithful, organizations)
Categorize conflicts by source
Analyze 3 conflict cases: Tokunbo's Eatery, Avocado Computers, Phelps Inc.
Read 'Four Phases of Collaborative Problem Solving'
Analyze 'Can Larry Fit In?' case study and write a collaborative role-play script

resources.slides: AEMP - Managing Conflicts for Mission - May 2026.pdf

topics.name: Motivating for Mission
assignments: Complete 'Motivating Others' Diagnostic Survey
Assess your competency in meeting motivation needs of others
Diagnose poor performance: identify obstacles and formulate improvement plan
Analyze 'Motivating Others' case study
Reflect on motivating vs demotivating aspects of your parish mission environment

resources.slides: AEMP - Motivating others for mission - May 2026.pdf

topics.name: Empowering for Mission
assignments: Complete 'Empowerment' Diagnostic Survey
Reflect on your level of empowerment and how you empower others
Differentiate between having power vs being empowered
Analyze 'Minding the Store' case study (Ken Hoffman & Ruth Cummings)
Identify violated empowerment guidelines and propose solutions

topics.name: Delegating for Mission
assignments: Complete 'Delegating' Diagnostic Survey
Analyze 'Delegating Case Study'
Differentiate delegation vs assignment (with examples)
Design a delegation plan for Harvest Planning Committee
Debate statements on delegation and motivation
Reflect on delegation as a tool for team development

topics.name: Leading and Building Teams and Groups for Mission
assignments: Complete 'Building Effective Teams' Diagnostic Survey
Complete Handouts 1-2 (Tasks - Leading Your Team)
Review Pastoral Competences and identify necessary skills
Complete Handouts 3-4 (Roles and responsibilities of team leader)
Read 'Group Roles and Trust Building' notes
Watch team building video (YouTube)
Complete Handouts 5, 6a, 6b (Building and developing a team)
Read 'Team Building Notes'

resources.slides: AEMP - Leading and Building Teams for Mission - 2026.pdf

topics.name: Conducting Effective Meetings for Mission
assignments: List all meetings you attend and their purposes
Read 'How to run a meeting' and 'Planning and Structuring Effective Meetings'
Reflect on challenges with meetings (Five Ps approach)
Identify effective vs ineffective participant behaviors
Read 'Managing Meeting Process' and 'Meeting Rules'
Analyze 'St. Albert Catholic Church' problem-solving case study

resources.slides: AEMP - Conducting Effective Meetings for Mission - May 2026.pdf

topics.name: Leading Positive Change for Mission
assignments: Complete 'Leading Positive Change' Diagnostic Survey
Recall a change effort in your parish - what worked and what could improve
Arm folding exercise - reflect on resistance to change
Complete all exercises from 'Leading Positive Change - Caselets and Exercises'

elections.description: 2026 Laity Elections - aligned with AEMP completion requirements

phasedApproach.description: Group elections for groups with 25+ members, aligned constitutions, and 3+ eligible members with min 4 AEMP scores
Parish Priest reviews outcomes and communicates next steps
Repeat process; eligible members need 18 AEMP scores to be voted for
Final elections phase; 18 AEMP scores required
Organizations submit executive AEMP status; vacant positions collated by PLC Elections Monitoring Committee
Official Commissioning Ceremony for all Parish leaders

whatsAppRules: No private messages or calls to admins (will not be responded to)
No complaints about attendance/scores on platforms (use Complaints Form)
Be on only one M&A platform at a time (exit all others)
Self-study participants: LFM facilitators not available for help
Parish groups may organize their own platforms for self-study members
LFM discontinuing WhatsApp for self-study participants at end of 2026

importantDates.quizDay: Sundays at 8pm-9:30pm (check calendar for exact dates)

parishGroups.groupStudyModel: Small groups of 6-16 members, with assigned discussion leaders
groupRequirements: Constitution aligned with AEMP Governance Guidelines
3+ eligible members with min 4 AEMP scores (for essential positions)

noteForMusicMinistry: The Spiritual Discernment modules (Strengths & Gifts) are especially relevant: 'Musicianship' is listed as a 'Leaves' category spiritual gift. The Gifts Discernment journaling and reflection exercises help you identify how your musical talent serves the mission of evangelization.

curriculum.description: 4-week Strengths Discernment journey based on CliftonStrengths talent themes. Full 20-page PDF extracted.

week1.title: Signature Themes - Discovering Your Talent Themes
exercises: Identify your Top 5 talent themes via Gallup assessment (Option 1: self-rate 34 themes 1-3; Option 2: purchase Gallup assessment)
Create your Signature Themes collage - write each theme on a separate page with the theme description, what it sounds like, what it looks like, and how it shows up in your life
Post 1 collage photo per theme (5 posts) in your group WhatsApp with a voice note or written reflection on each

week2.title: Living Your Strengths - Making Your Talents Productive
exercises: Read the description of each of your 5 themes on Gallup's website and watch the associated theme webinar
Identify what you do in your daily life that already reflects each talent theme (at least 3 examples per theme)
Identify new ways you could use each talent theme to serve your family, workplace, parish, and community
Post about the insights from one theme each day (Week 2 = Mon-Fri, 1 theme per day)

week3.title: Developing Your Strengths - Aware & Proactive Use of Your Talents
exercises: Answer for each of your 5 themes: What would happen if I used this talent more? What would happen if I used this talent less?
Identify one action you can take this week for each theme to use it more effectively
For each theme: describe a person you know who exemplifies this talent well and what you can learn from them
Complete the Strengths Journaling Template (XLSX) for Week 3 daily reflections

week4.title: Sustaining Your Strengths - Integration for Mission
exercises: Complete an integration exercise: how your 5 themes work together as a team inside you
Identify potential blind spots of each theme (overuse or misuse)
Write your Strengths Partnership Plan: identify who complements your strengths (someone whose top themes differ from yours)
Write your personal mission statement integrating your top 5 talent themes
Post: 'My Top 5 Strengths and my mission statement' as your final post

curriculum.submissions: Weekly submissions posted to group WhatsApp (5 posts Week 1, daily Mon-Fri Weeks 2-4)

capstone.title: Strengths Partnership Plan
capstone.description: Final exercise: pair up with someone whose strengths complement yours, create a joint plan for collaborative mission

gallupCliftonStrengths.description: 34 talent themes organized into 4 domains. Used to identify your Top 5 natural talents for leadership and mission.

executing.description: People who know how to make things happen

influencing.description: People who know how to take charge, speak up, and make sure others are heard

relationshipBuilding.description: People who build strong relationships that hold a team together

strategicThinking.description: People who absorb and analyze information to make better decisions

selfAssessmentSheet.title: 34 CliftonStrengths Self-Rating
selfAssessmentSheet.format: Rate each of the 34 themes as 1 (not me), 2 (maybe), or 3 (definitely me)

extractedPdfContent.location: aemp/extracted/ directory (JSON files with full text)

parishPastoralLaityCouncils.summary: Official Archdiocese of Lagos Parish Pastoral & Laity Councils Guidelines. Covers: purpose/functions of PPC, consultative role, core pastoral areas, councilor responsibilities, membership, selection, tenure, meeting protocols, relationship between PPC and PLC.

parishFinanceAdmin.summary: Parish Finance Council & Administration Guidelines. Covers: consultative body to Parish Priest, recommended consultation areas, membership, officers, conflicts of interest, tenure, meeting protocols, committees.

humanResources.summary: Human Resources Management Guidelines (Revised 2023). Covers: HR policies for clergy, lay employees, and volunteers; background on missionary mandate (Matt 28:19) and Vatican II.

deaneryPastoralCouncil.summary: Deanery Pastoral Council Guidelines (Oct 2019). Mission: coordinate pastoral activities of parishes for managing representation and sharing best practices.

deaneryLaityCouncil.summary: Deanery Laity Council Guidelines (Oct 2019). Charged by Apostolicam Actuositatem with intensifying apostolic activity of laity.

lalcGuidelines.summary: Archdiocesan Laity Council Guidelines (Aug 2022). Charged with promoting solidarity and mutual support among deanery councils.

stThomasWinneba.summary: St. Thomas Catholic Church - Father Kofi's inclusive leadership built community (parish house, church building, harvest growth). Conflict arose when he began consulting only one parishioner (Mr. Mensah), leading to drastic harvest decline and reduced participation.

ekaDeanery.summary: Eka Deanery Laity Council Chairperson issued directives: 'Year of Praying for Dead' with mandatory prayers, N500/member levy split with Archdiocesan Council, delegate limits. Presidents questioned alignment with Archbishop's guidelines.

mabel.summary: Mabel (22, graduate) works long hours (4:30am-8pm), active in parish (CYON, Choir, Sacred Heart, Block Rosary, Deanery Secretary), plus online courses. Shows stress signs: isolation, slower work, crying, irritability, emotional outbursts.

stPetersChurch.summary: St. Peter's lost 1/3 of 1500 parishioners due to heat, bad PA system, poor altar visibility. PPC Vice Chairman Chief Okoye led renovation (AC, screens, nursing area, PA system) funded by 10th anniversary, bringing parishioners back.

newPlcChairman.summary: Austin Ajao (40, 5 yrs in parish, only YCP experience) elected PLC Chairman with 15 voters. Within a year: meeting attendance lowest, activities sparse, fund-raising failed. Blamed minority state status; Parish Priest unsure how to handle constitutionally elected but ineffective leader.

canLarryFitIn.summary: Conflict management exercise with scenarios: Tokunbo's Eatery (family, smoke allergy), Avocado Computers (lazy colleague), Phelps Inc. (workload equity). Uses Table 4 to match strategies to situational factors.

motivatingOthers.summary: Shade Sambo case: single parent, consistently 10-20 min late, manager Andy Taiwo must address performance using reshaping techniques. Includes role-play instructions.

mindingTheStore.summary: Ruth Cummings, new branch manager, hired admin assistant at above-market rate. Boss Ken Hoffman questioned her autonomy. Includes empowerment/delegation analysis.

delegating.summary: Kunle, building committee chairman of St. Dennis Church, under pressure to deliver outstation church building in 8 months. Must delegate due to work travel. Includes delegation planning worksheets.

stAlbertChurch.summary: Fr. Ugboko, new Dean, gave PPC Vice Chairman Chief Okodudu increased responsibility. While Fr. was on vacation, Chief signed renovation contract, bought generator, donated parish funds to outstation. Question: PPC or PFC handles this?

leadingPositiveChange.summary: Fr. Okon's new parish: PPC/PFC not met in 2 years, PLC led removal of past PP, families not speaking, finances in disarray. Uses AEMP exercises (Reflected Best Self) with parish leaders. Includes 14 caselets on change scenarios.

spiritualGifts.summary: Full descriptions of spiritual gifts: Administration, Craftsmanship, Digital Media, Encouragement, Evangelization, Faith, Giving, Healing, Helps, Hospitality, Intercessory Prayer, Knowledge, Leadership, Mercy, Music Ministry, Pastoring, Prophecy, Service, Teaching, Wisdom. Each with Scripture references, CCC references, patron saint, and possible parish roles.

handouts.lookingThroughTheGlass: Self-awareness exercise: share assessment scores with group, complete 10 sentence stems about strengths, weaknesses, success, priorities, and impressions of others.
handouts.modelOfProblemSolving: 4 pages combining St. Peter's case study with problem-solving model handout.

principles: 1. Congruent, not incongruent — honest messages where verbal statements match thoughts and feelings
2. Descriptive, not evaluative — describe objective occurrence, your reaction, and offer alternative
3. Problem-oriented, not person-oriented — focus on issues that can be changed, not people
4. Validating, not invalidating — communicate respect, flexibility, collaboration, agreement
5. Specific, not global — focus on specific events/behaviors, avoid extreme or either-or statements
6. Conjunctive, not disjunctive — statements that flow from previous and facilitate interaction
7. Owned, not disowned — take responsibility using personal 'I' words
8. Supportive listening, not one-way listening — use varied responses with bias toward reflective
supportiveCommunication.source: D.A. Whetten and K.S. Cameron, 2007. Developing Management Skills

handouts.collaborativeProblemSolving: 10 pages covering 4 phases: problem identification, solution generation, action plan formulation, implementation/follow-up. Detailed guidance for initiator, responder, and mediator roles.
handouts.selfAwarenessCase: 5 pages: Healthcare director's story - lost superstar manager due to underperforming peer; used 360 evaluations and data but manager explained away all feedback.
handouts.howToRunAMeeting: 9 pages: James Ware classic reading on meeting effectiveness - setting objectives, selecting participants, agenda planning, problem-solving encouragement, decision-making.
handouts.planningEffectiveMeetings: 4 pages: Components (content, interaction, process), achieving objectives, chairperson roles.
handouts.managingMeetingProcess: 2 pages: Exercise identifying task-facilitating, relationship-building, and blocking roles in EXCO.
handouts.meetingRules: 1 page: 10 rules from meetingsift.com - participate, let everyone participate, listen with open mind, think before speaking, stay on point, attack the problem not the person, close decisions, record outcomes.
handouts.pastoralCompetences: 3 pages: Catholic Leadership 360 for Lay Leaders - maps Church sources (Co-Workers in the Vineyard, Certification Standards, Excellence Standards, De Lambo study) to 99 research-based competencies.
handouts.groupRoles: 8 pages: Benne and Sheats' 26 group roles - task roles, personal/social roles, dysfunctional/individualistic roles.
handouts.teamBuilding: 5 pages: Tuckman's stages - Forming, Storming, Norming, Performing. Leader tasks and effective behaviors for each stage.
handouts.lalcExtraReading: 3 pages: Canon Law norms for canonical elections - eligibility, active/passive voice, voting validity, majority requirements.

spiritualDiscernmentJourneys.description: Prayer journey programme referenced in the Strengths materials; separate from AEMP but complementary
guidelinesSummary: Non-attendance at first session results in removal and deferral to next batch
Timely submission, commitment, openness, honesty, sincerity required
Late/non-submissions lead to removal unless reason communicated
Confidentiality of posts and group conversations expected
Vulnerability encourages healing but share only what is comfortable
All communication via WhatsApp groups (no private chats to mentors)
Confession and pastoral counselling recommended alongside the journey
Voluntary withdrawal allowed; discuss with mentors first (may be resistance)
Body-mind-spirit connection; psychological issues referred to psychotherapist (Chichi)
Subsidiarity: reach out to group members before mentors
Group norms: respectful, considerate, supportive; disruptive behaviour = removal
